title: EventLog Entry
description: Schema defining the structure of an EventLog entry within the Agent OS. EventLog is the single immutable, append-only source of truth for all meaningful events across systems. Every event must support replay, auditing, deduplication, and historical reconstruction.

event_type.description: Classification of the event (e.g., governance_rule_added, tangent_added, file_updated, file_created, file_deleted, decision, approval, veto).

category.description: Higher-level category for grouping events (e.g., governance, implementation, system, human, maintenance).

source_system.description: The system that generated or captured this event (e.g., Cody, Warp, orchestrator, git, pipeline).

producer_id.description: Unique identifier of the agent, human, or system that produced this event.

idempotency_key.description: Unique key ensuring this event is processed exactly once, even if replayed.

hash.description: Cryptographic hash of the event payload for integrity verification.

raw_payload.description: The original, unmodified event data as captured by the source system.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the event was recorded.

actor.description: Human-readable name or identifier of the entity that triggered the event.

correlation_id.description: ID linking this event to a related conversation thread or workflow.

metadata.description: Additional structured metadata not captured in other fields.

links.description: References to related governance files, tangents, or events.

parent_event_id.description: ID of the parent event this one derives from.

related_governance.description: List of constitution file paths related to this event.

related_tangents.description: List of tangent IDs related to this event.
